response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems developer with a strong track record in web infrastructure and test-driven development, but he does not meet the core requirements for this role. The position demands 8 years of C++ experience as a primary qualification; the resume lists only a brief ~6-month stint using C and C++ at National Instruments, and C++ does not appear in his skills section at all. His infrastructure background skews heavily toward web services, micro-services, and application-layer tooling rather than cloud compute platforms, distributed systems hardware architecture, or storage technologies. There is also no evidence of sustained technical leadership over cloud or DevOps engineering teams.
strengths: Genuine distributed-systems exposure at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O techniques
Consistent track record of knowledge-sharing: organized Refactoring Dojos, delivered conference talks, and built testing frameworks — indicative of mentoring instincts
Polyglot engineer comfortable across embedded, web, mobile, and DevOps tooling, showing adaptability and breadth
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months vs. the 8-year minimum); C++ is not even listed in his skills section, making this a hard disqualifier
No demonstrated experience with cloud compute platform internals, hardware architecture, storage systems, or networking at the infrastructure layer required by this role
No formal technical lead experience managing cloud or DevOps engineers; staff titles at Altom and re:cinq reflect seniority level, not sustained leadership of platform-focused engineering teams
